title: MANGA Evangelion Official Guidebook by Sadamoto
display_title: MANGA Evangelion Official Guidebook by Sadamoto
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Manga Official Guidebook             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:58 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: General Reminder : Please don't bump this thread unless it's actual news (or transcript) related to a new manga stage .

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 01, 2011 3:45 pm I gave this its own thread, might as well since it's a new product. :) Rei's eyes look somewhat off (Shinji's and Asuka's don't) in that illustration... Unusual, considering it is a Sadamoto image. This guidebook seems interesting, but I don't understand why Sadamoto would release a manga-based guidebook now (other than to make some quick money?) when his story's not even done yet. Wouldn't it make more sense to release this after his manga is complete? Some questions: Does the guidebook go into at all how Kaworu=Adam and Rei=Lilith or anything along those lines? Anything about Shinji's thoughts up to the latest Stage? Or any "hints" of where Sadamoto's version of EoE is going? Thanks! (Speaking of the manga, isn't the newest Stage supposed to come out this month or next month? Frustrating to think that it was supposed to come out in "winter" of this year and still nothing.)

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri Apr 01, 2011 7:29 pm Do want.... Also, any news on a new Sadamoto artbook?

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Apr 01, 2011 11:03 pm This is the most recent one that came out. It will be a while before there is another one.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Fri Apr 01, 2011 11:18 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This guidebook seems interesting, but I don't understand why Sadamoto would release a manga-based guidebook now (other than to make some quick money?) when his story's not even done yet. Wouldn't it make more sense to release this after his manga is complete?

[]: Evangelion Manga Official Guidebook - 2             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Apr 01, 2011 11:30 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This guidebook seems interesting, but I don't understand why Sadamoto would release a manga-based guidebook now (other than to make some quick money?) when his story's not even done yet. Wouldn't it make more sense to release this after his manga is complete?

[]:             Quote Post by gordanham » Wed Apr 06, 2011 10:28 pm Thanks for the nice thread. Though I don't think guidebooks sale well in the states, I would really love it to be released here. Though they are repetitive and regurgitative, I am addicted to guide books. It is just a shame that usually only really popular series get them, and them being so popular and all, the guidebooks' formation is usually never done by the actual creator, but assistants. Were there any color pages too? And I hate to be a nuisance again, but do you have any idea what these monthly Eva publications are? http://www.amazon.co.jp/exec/obidos/ASIN/4777808866/evafan-22/ref=nosim/ Though there seems to be two series of them, it was this one with the Rebuild version of Eva-04 that really got me wondering what they were.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Apr 06, 2011 10:59 pm gordanham wrote: Were there any color pages too?

[]:             Quote Post by gordanham » Thu Apr 07, 2011 7:57 pm Haha. Thank you. Now after seeing it again I can't believe I didn't figure that out. I even have some Evangelion pachinko manuals myslef. Though they are of absolute no use to me, I love the one I have for the same reason I was interested in this one, Unit-04 is all over it! I don't know why I like Unit-04 exactly so much, but I am really envious that it and Kaworu have such a bigger roll in the Pachinko games. When I went to Japan I actually got to see and entire Eva Pachinko parlor. That was before the I got the guides though. Thank you again for your help.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 01, 2011 3:45 pm I gave this its own thread, might as well since it&#3
